title: About Shinji piano learning skills
wikitext:
[]: About Shinji piano learning skills             Quote Post by cpt. Amarao » Fri Jul 05, 2013 11:00 am This is my second post here, yay! I have read the faq about 3.33 and i think that its a great job, it helped me a lot to understand the film, and i wanted to contribute with a theory over the Shinji piano skills, i have read the angel powers theory and i know that this is not the biggest mistery but every bit counts. Here are my two cents, In chapter 15 from original series, Asuka returns home and finds Shinji playing his violoncello, Shinji tells Asuka that he has been learning music from 5, that is almost 10 years receiving music lessons, that is a good base to learn how to play a single piece over any music instrument faster than somebody without a trained ear, in addition he could played the piano before during his music lessons, but due to his insecurities he started playing slowly afraid to do something wrong or shameful, c'mon it's Shinji! As an alternative/complementary theory maybe he has unusual learning skills, like autistics kids (it's Shinji again!), at least he could make eva-01 to take a few steps without beforehand practice. But we know one thing for sure, he can (not) learn how to avoid Impacts

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Jul 05, 2013 11:11 am Shinji playing the cello is only confirmed for the NGE continuity, and that was not even a passion of him, but something his teacher made him learn and that he didn't stopped because "nobody told him to". In NME he does have an hobby : astronomy, but nothing hinting that he plays music(we didn't saw any case for an instrument in his room in 1.0 or 2.0), besides when Kaworu invites him to play the duet Shinji flat-out said that he don't know to play the piano... and after 20 seconds is magically able to play a perfect duet with a guy that probably didn't had nothing else to do but to play piano for the past 14 years! I'm sure there is some angelic funk at play here!

[]:             Quote Post by TruthfulLie » Fri Jul 05, 2013 11:26 am He probably has some sort of musical talent which enables him to learn quickly, but I believe the scene was mere abstraction of his learning process and passage of time. Nothing more. I think the scene makes it rather clear that he is learning because of the way his playing starts off. He is using only his index fingers as if someone who doesn't know how to play the piano then gradually he starts to utilize other fingers and finally all ten of them. The idea of him already knowing how to play seems far fetched to me since we don't see any music sheet during the first duet. Even any skilled player couldn't randomly start playing a piece that he doesn't know and there are no clear indication pointing that he already knew the piece either.

[]:             Quote Post by cpt. Amarao » Fri Jul 05, 2013 11:34 am i know that, i said that its a possibility because in the original he plays. About Shinji saying he doesnt know how to play the piano could be because he thinks that he do it bad and he is going to perform poorly, the same with him starting to play only with two fingers, insecurity. And there are people who can play a piece listening it only one time, but i dont think Shinji can. Or maybe its an angel, the mages/nanomachines of evangelion. ;) If its a longer period of time, its poorly displayed IMHO.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Jul 05, 2013 11:45 am The problem with that is that there is some hints that at must only few days have passed between Shinji arriving and the battle in Lilith's Chamber : - Shinji asked for Kaworu to repair his SDAT the day before everything has gone to hell for him(i.e. when Kaworu showed him the outside world), since Shinji plays the damn thing all the time when he has more than one minute with nothing to do, I doubt he waited weeks to ask for Kaworu to fix it. - Every night he brought to Rei new books with the hope that she will start to read them, the pile at the end suggested that he made this "offering" 4-5 times, and I highly doubt that it took him weeks to find it really odd that Rei don't read them. - When Kaworu and Shinji activated EVA-13, an alarm in the Wunder immediately started to ring, probably because of the DSS Choker that Kaworu was wearing. And since Asuka and Mari arived almost at the same time that EVA-13 and Mark.09, it means that the Wunder was on stand by around neo-NERV territory, waiting for Gendo to make the first move. I really doubt that WILLE waited for weeks flying around! - Kaworu and Shinji got on a first name basis only when the former convinced the latter to pilot EVA-13, spending weeks being almost the whole day together playing piano and gazing at the stars and only starting to calls themselves by their first name at the end? Unlikely. For me, everything points that Shinji spent only a few days in neo-NERV, besides that would be a nice parallel with NGE, where Kaworu was only around for few days before having to be killed, reinforced the tragedy that they can't be together for more than a few days before tragedy strikes. The creators have pushed the vice to the point of putting Shinji and Kaworu in the official poster for 3.0 respectively under the Vega and Altair star and with the Milkyway running between them, a direct reference to the legend for the star-crossed lovers. ... guy, you just gave me an idea for a new topic, this needs to be discussed!

[]:             Quote Post by TruthfulLie » Fri Jul 05, 2013 1:12 pm What you are suggesting isn't based on what we see but rather what we feel about the situation. We can't distinguish this ambiguity from what is "likely" and what isn't. That's not objective. By that standard, I could argue exactly the opposite. Shinji being a timid person could very well have waited around for quite some time. Him actually confronting Kaoru about SDAT so quickly is unlikely. The library was complete wreck and finding English books could have been difficult for Shinji especially when the library was dark as it seemed. The process could've taken even longer time due to such limitations. And perhaps he was looking for a specific book, Little Prince and simply brought Rei other English books when he couldn't find it. Wille could very well have floated around for quite some time and very likely because they certainly needed the time to repair unit 02 with its new parts and equipment. This operation could've taken more than mere few days. Going first name basis for Japanese is something different than Western culture. They could've known for years and still be on last name basis. You get my point. I'm actually not suggesting that weeks or months have passed or trying to disprove anything, but simply suggesting the possibility of abstraction of the scene itself. Such abstraction doesn't necessarily mean longer period of time like weeks. It could be just few days which is more probable. But can he learn the songs just within few days without some super-human angelic powers? I certainly think so. People may think that it may take weeks to learn the pieces they've played but think about this. They had nothing else to do. A couple days of practice for say...nearly entire day, should be enough for Shinji to learn the song. He's hitting all the right notes, and simply hitting the right notes could be achieved within days if practiced hard, especially if someone has musical talent which is what I was suggesting. Of course it won't be perfect and Shinji even mentions this himself saying how we could play better.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Jul 05, 2013 1:55 pm I don't care what divine talent or deal with the devil someone has, nobody can learn to play the piano like that in the minute or so after sitting down, for the first time ever (if that's actually the case), without having made any mistakes. Mozart couldn't learn to play the piano in a couple of minutes. The piece itself could be learned that quickly, if you had a good ear, a good memory, and know enough about theory, but chances are, he'd have to have heard the piece before. The idea that Shinji may have had formal training is probably the most reasonable, if we're taking that scene literally. You usually end up with some rudimentary lessons on piano if you're focus is on another instrument. Practice rooms always have a piano in them. But we've not been given any indication that this is the case in rebuild.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Jul 05, 2013 2:38 pm Playing the bass clef of a piano isn't really that complicated, though it is odd that Shinji started hitting a few decent sounding block chords at the first try. Maybe he learned some music before, but it's never suggested that he did any of the cello stuff in NME. My half-assed guess is that Kaworu is syncing up with Shinji in ways that effect Shinji's playing abilities, like how Kaworu was able to sync up with Unit 02 so quickly. But then, he was only able to do that in NGE because Unit 02's soul was hiding herself. The only one suggested to have a misplaced soul is Rei, and none of the side-effects she seems to get from that are found anywhere in Shinji.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Jul 05, 2013 3:15 pm This is far, far beyond simply the bass clef of a piece, which usually consists of a rhythm and/or supporting chords and notes. This is a 4 hands piece, so the lower 2 hands is actually doing something far more complex than a supporting role , it takes over melodically, switching with the other 2 hands in the higher registers. He starts with this: And 30 seconds (literally, 30 seconds) later: (apologies for the gifs, using mplayer's gif output device and it doesn't always handle the colorspace correctly when changing image modes) All of this without sheet music.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Jul 05, 2013 7:23 pm ElMariachi wrote: Shinji playing the cello is only confirmed for the NGE continuity, and that was not even a passion of him, but something his teacher made him learn and that he didn't stopped because "nobody told him to".

[]:             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Fri Jul 05, 2013 8:59 pm Kendrix wrote: That's what he says so Asuka won't make fun of him. He always gives these sort-of half-hearted answers. ("Misato...? Uh, I... don't dislike her." When we all know that's a huge understatement. )

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Fri Jul 05, 2013 11:58 pm *Observes Ornette's evidence* Whoa! I just checked my copy to make sure, and indeed, a plain reading of that scene suggests Shinji went from that to that in the same sitting . I had a mistake in my memory about when certain shots occur between the two piano scenes (I forgot there even were two, very close together). kuribo-04 wrote: Maybe as some people of a video review that I saw said, he learns playing with the "power of gay or sexual confusion"...

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Jul 06, 2013 12:24 am Kendrix wrote: Knowing the piece wouldn't be that much of a problem, doesn't Kaworu have a music sheet in front of him, like ppl with instruments usually do?

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Sat Jul 06, 2013 1:31 am My upright piano's music rack is actually on the inside of the fallboard, but that's beside the point. If that really was just one sitting of practice with no prior experience - and it does look that way at this point - then the only explanation I can come up with is "Bishounen Space Jesus Magic". Kaworu's purpose was to push Shinji towards piloting Eva of his own free will, right? If that's the case, then artificially building up Shinji's self-confidence seems like a good place to start.

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Sat Jul 06, 2013 2:09 am Yeah, another vote from me on the abstract take: http://forum.evageeks.org/post/633927/Evangelion-333-FAQ/#633927 Blue Monday wrote: Should probably be noted that the scene is obviously an abstraction. Shinji couldn't be playing 'Quatre Mains' near perfeclty in the first session as the movie shows. The implication or impression is of a passing of time; that this is the end result of days, possibly even weeks, of practise - Hence the last cut with Shinji mimicking the keys in his room; he knows the piece almost by heart now.

[]:             Quote Post by TruthfulLie » Sat Jul 06, 2013 2:38 am They have the sheet during the rainbow seq. It's strange considering they didn't have one during the first sequence and based purely on the way scene is directed (without taking context of Shinji's skills), the first sequence feels more real while the second feels more abstracted and montage-like with dialogues, imageries and all. Yet realistic details such as sheets says the other way around.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sat Jul 06, 2013 4:54 am I am perfectly happy with the argument that Shinji is at Nerv for only a week or so; but this gives plenty of time for the piano scene still to be viewed as an abstraction - which is how it's felt to me from the first time I saw it. And absolute realism in the learning process is in any case simply unnecessary in a story that has so many fanciful and totally unrealistic elements.

[]:             Quote Post by Sgt. Griff » Sat Jul 06, 2013 6:26 am Maybe he's just got average improv skills, good eyes, and a good ear? The piece seems to be largely composed by the two while they play it. just my $0.02

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Sat Jul 06, 2013 6:32 am pwhodges wrote: I am perfectly happy with the argument that Shinji is at Nerv for only a week or so; but this gives plenty of time for the piano scene still to be viewed as an abstraction - which is how it's felt to me from the first time I saw it.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Sat Jul 06, 2013 7:21 am Shinji suddenly becoming Amadeus on the piano is a little jarring at first but I've never let it really irk me. We only see him "train" how to pilot the Eva once & it involves him standing in one place & "Center the target. Pull the switch". The kid has got some bit of a prodigy in him. Plus I love the piano scene soooooo much I'd rather have it then a series of vignettes showing Shinji get a wee bit better each time.
